85	func TestLintHook_UsesRunnerLibrary(t *testing.T) {
86		root := newLintHookTestRoot(t)
87		store, b := newLintHookTestStore(t, root)
88	
89		runner := &lintHookRunnerStub{}
90		runner.run = func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
91			return &Result{
92				ExitCode: 0,
93				Output:   `{"score":7,"rationale":"ok","suggested_fixes":["none"],"waivers_applied":[]}`,
94			}, nil
95		}
96	
97		hook := NewPreDispatchLintHook(root, store, lintHookTestConfig(), nil, runner)
98		got, err := hook(context.Background(), b.ID)
99		require.NoError(t, err)
100		assert.Equal(t, 7, got.Score)
101	
102		assert.Equal(t, "bead-lifecycle-lint", runner.lastOpts.PromptSource)
103		assert.Equal(t, root, runner.lastOpts.WorkDir)
104		assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "MODE: lint")
105		assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, "bead-lifecycle")
106		assert.Contains(t, runner.lastOpts.Prompt, `"spec-id": "FEAT-999"`)
107	
108		_, thisFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
109		require.True(t, ok)
110		lintHookPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(thisFile), "lint_hook.go")
111		fset := token.NewFileSet()
112		parsed, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, lintHookPath, nil, parser.ImportsOnly)
113		require.NoError(t, err)
114		for _, imp := range parsed.Imports {
115			assert.NotEqual(t, `"os/exec"`, imp.Path.Value, "lint_hook.go must not import os/exec")
116		}
117		body, err := os.ReadFile(lintHookPath)
118		require.NoError(t, err)
119		assert.NotContains(t, string(body), "ddx agent run", "lint_hook.go must not shell out to ddx agent run")
120	}

18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
